reasoning: The resolution criteria require NASA's CNEOS to estimate a 5% or greater chance of asteroid 2024 YR4 impacting Earth by June 30, 2025. Multiple sources indicate that the probability for 2024 YR4 peaked at 3.1% on February 18, 2025. Following this, new observations from the Very Large Telescope and others allowed astronomers to significantly refine its orbit, and by late February 2025, the impact probability dropped to virtually zero (around 0.005%). It has since been completely ruled out as a threat to Earth. Once an asteroid's orbit is resolved well enough to confidently drop its impact probability to zero, it does not bounce back up to 5%. In addition, the Polymarket price for this market dropped to 0.00 by mid-June 2025, confirming the consensus that this event will not happen. Since it has already been ruled out without ever hitting the 5% threshold, the likelihood of resolving YES is astronomically low.
